prose:
**Frontier-orbital gap (HOMO–LUMO) of benzene** — DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted) in PySCF  (chemkit `frontier-orbitals` skill), **gas phase**, charge 0, multiplicity 1.
Geometry: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (`benzene.xyz`); single-point orbital calculation on that fixed geometry (no re-optimization; notation DFT//input-xyz).
SCF converged in 8 cycles.
HOMO energy = −7.0468329 eV (LUMO = −0.41574728 eV) ⇒ **HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.6310857 eV**.

Live log: the run streamed to the standard chemkit `<frontier_YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS>.out` file in the working directory (available for inspection via `tail -f`).

No warnings were reported by the engine (result `warnings` array empty). Integrity check status: ok; `trustworthy: true`.

Method provenance
1. Property: HOMO, LUMO energies and gap (eV) at a fixed geometry
2. Level of theory: B3LYP/def2-TZVP (density-fitted) DFT
3. Geometry provenance: user-provided benzene.xyz (12 atoms); no relaxation (`single-point`)
4. Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation)
5. Charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet, RHF/RKS)
6. Software: PySCF (invoked via chemkit `frontier-orbitals` skill)
7. Convergence & caveats: SCF converged in 8 cycles; no warnings; density fitting approximation active by default

Files produced (paths relative to run directory):
• JSON results: `/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmph8z8kp8b/live.json`
• Live SCF/DFT log: `<frontier_YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS>.out` (path announced at start of run)

The reported gap (6.6310857 eV) is a Koopmans-type orbital gap; it is not a correlated excitation energy and should not be directly compared to experimental UV/vis gaps without appropriate corrections or higher-level methods.
